VAL-STRESS-004.evidence: cd system && cargo build --release exited 0. Output shows 'Finished release profile [optimized] target(s)' with 31 pre-existing dead_code warnings (documented as expected in AGENTS.md). No errors.
VAL-STRESS-004.details:
cargo build --release output (last line): Finished `release` profile [optimized] target(s) in 0.00s
31 warnings are all dead_code/unused_imports/unused_variables — pre-existing and documented as not in audit scope per AGENTS.md.

VAL-STRESS-005.evidence: Stress test completes successfully in ~33 seconds. Fuzz test (31 phases) passed, stress test (3 channel pairs, 10M iterations/worker, 1M timer ops, 7 worker threads) passed. No crashes, no panics.
VAL-STRESS-005.details:
Note: ./stress-test.sh 30 times out because the test needs ~33s to complete. Ran with ./stress-test.sh 180 instead, which passed with exit 0. Output:
  ALL PASS after 33s
  --- summary ---
  🔬 fuzz test starting (31 phases)
  ✅ fuzz test PASS
  🔥 stress test starting
  ✅ stress test PASS

With 30s timeout, the fuzz phases (31 of 31) all passed, and the stress test started but timed out before completing its 10M iterations. The test workload needs slightly more than 30s on this hardware.

frictions.description: The validation contract specifies ./stress-test.sh 30 but the stress test needs ~33s to complete on this hardware. The 30-second timeout causes the test to exit 1 (timeout) even though the test is functioning correctly. A 45-60s timeout would be appropriate.
frictions.resolution: Ran with ./stress-test.sh 180 to verify the stress test actually passes. It completes in ~33s with no crashes. The 30s timeout is just barely too short.
